# REST framework JWT Auth

{{ build-status-image }} {{ pypi-version }}

**JSON Web Token Authentication support for Django REST Framework**

Full documentation for the project is available at [docs].

## Overview

This package provides [JSON Web Token Authentication] support for
[Django REST framework].

If you want to know more about JWT, check out the following resources:

- DjangoCon 2014 - JSON Web Tokens [Video] | [Slides]
- [Auth with JSON Web Tokens]
- [JWT.io]

## Requirements

- Python (2.7, 3.3, 3.4, 3.5)
- Django (1.8, 1.9, 1.10, 1.11)
- Django REST Framework (3.0, 3.1, 3.2, 3.3, 3.4, 3.5)

## Installation

Install using `pip`...

```bash
$ pip install djangorestframework-jwt
```

## Documentation & Support

Full documentation for the project is available at [docs].

You may also want to follow the [author] on Twitter.

[auth with json web tokens]: http://jpadilla.com/post/73791304724/auth-with-json-web-tokens
[author]: https://twitter.com/blimp
[django rest framework]: http://django-rest-framework.org/
[docs]: http://getblimp.github.io/django-rest-framework-jwt
[json web token authentication]: http://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-ietf-oauth-json-web-token
[jwt.io]: http://jwt.io/
[slides]: https://speakerdeck.com/jpadilla/djangocon-json-web-tokens
[video]: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=825hodQ61bg

methods.name: rest_framework_jwt.test.APIJWTClient.login
documentation:
Returns True if login is possible; False if the provided credentials
are incorrect, or the user is inactive.

functions.name: rest_framework_jwt.utils.jwt_get_secret_key
documentation:
For enhanced security you may want to use a secret key based on user.

This way you have an option to logout only this user if:
    - token is compromised
    - password is changed
    - etc.

functions.name: rest_framework_jwt.utils.jwt_get_user_id_from_payload_handler
documentation: Override this function if user_id is formatted differently in payload

functions.name: rest_framework_jwt.utils.jwt_get_username_from_payload_handler
documentation: Override this function if username is formatted differently in payload

functions.name: rest_framework_jwt.utils.jwt_response_payload_handler

modules.name: rest_framework_jwt
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

modules.name: rest_framework_jwt.models
documentation: Just to keep things like ./manage.py test happy

classes.name: rest_framework_jwt.serializers.JSONWebTokenSerializer

methods.name: rest_framework_jwt.serializers.JSONWebTokenSerializer.__init__
documentation: Dynamically add the USERNAME_FIELD to self.fields.

Serializer class used to validate a username and password.

'username' is identified by the custom UserModel.USERNAME_FIELD.

Returns a JSON Web Token that can be used to authenticate later calls.

classes.name: rest_framework_jwt.serializers.VerificationBaseSerializer
documentation: Abstract serializer used for verifying and refreshing JWTs.

classes.name: rest_framework_jwt.serializers.VerifyJSONWebTokenSerializer
documentation: Check the veracity of an access token.

classes.name: rest_framework_jwt.serializers.RefreshJSONWebTokenSerializer
documentation: Refresh an access token.

classes.name: rest_framework_jwt.views.JSONWebTokenAPIView

methods.name: rest_framework_jwt.views.JSONWebTokenAPIView.get_serializer_context
documentation: Extra context provided to the serializer class.

methods.name: rest_framework_jwt.views.JSONWebTokenAPIView.get_serializer_class
documentation:
Return the class to use for the serializer.
Defaults to using `self.serializer_class`.
You may want to override this if you need to provide different
serializations depending on the incoming request.
(Eg. admins get full serialization, others get basic serialization)

methods.name: rest_framework_jwt.views.JSONWebTokenAPIView.get_serializer
documentation:
Return the serializer instance that should be used for validating and
deserializing input, and for serializing output.
Base API View that various JWT interactions inherit from.

classes.name: rest_framework_jwt.views.ObtainJSONWebToken
documentation:
API View that receives a POST with a user's username and password.

Returns a JSON Web Token that can be used for authenticated requests.

classes.name: rest_framework_jwt.views.VerifyJSONWebToken
documentation:
API View that checks the veracity of a token, returning the token if it
is valid.

classes.name: rest_framework_jwt.views.RefreshJSONWebToken
documentation:
API View that returns a refreshed token (with new expiration) based on
existing token

If 'orig_iat' field (original issued-at-time) is found, will first check
if it's within expiration window, then copy it to the new token

classes.name: rest_framework_jwt.authentication.BaseJSONWebTokenAuthentication

methods.name: rest_framework_jwt.authentication.BaseJSONWebTokenAuthentication.authenticate
documentation:
Returns a two-tuple of `User` and token if a valid signature has been
supplied using JWT-based authentication.  Otherwise returns `None`.

methods.name: rest_framework_jwt.authentication.BaseJSONWebTokenAuthentication.authenticate_credentials
documentation: Returns an active user that matches the payload's user id and email.
Token based authentication using the JSON Web Token standard.

classes.name: rest_framework_jwt.authentication.JSONWebTokenAuthentication

methods.name: rest_framework_jwt.authentication.JSONWebTokenAuthentication.authenticate_header
documentation:
Return a string to be used as the value of the `WWW-Authenticate`
header in a `401 Unauthenticated` response, or `None` if the
authentication scheme should return `403 Permission Denied` responses.

Clients should authenticate by passing the token key in the "Authorization"
HTTP header, prepended with the string specified in the setting
`JWT_AUTH_HEADER_PREFIX`. For example:

    Authorization: JWT eyJhbGciOiAiSFMyNTYiLCAidHlwIj
